title: Finally rebuild movies are 3 or 4
wikitext:
[]: Finally, rebuild movies are 3 or 4?             Quote Post by AkiraT » Wed Mar 09, 2011 3:38 pm Hi today I got this doubt, that I know has always talked about 4 movies. not 3, a trilogy. However, in a recent interview that I found on youtube at the Italian head of Dynit, Carlo Cavazzoni (Italian distributors of evangelion) created last October at the fair of Lucca Comics and Games, as he presented the dvd and bluray of evangelion 2.22, has repeatedly said that this is a trilogy, 3 films, not 4. and he, of course, has some contacts with the Khara studio. Here you can see the nice video of the fair with the interview: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7X0VjPQO1bU&hd=1 (He speaks at 3:25) Maybe it was wrong, but it's strange. Now, I knew that the film was 4. after "Q" there must be a "Final"... is it possible that Anno wants to group all in one movie? would be very bad edit: oops I forgot that the interview is in Italian, here I put the English transcript (there may be some error): [...]and then we also have the second movie of evangelion, eagerly awaited, both in DVD and Blu-ray, is the second film in the trilogy, the new trilogy of Evangelion, which goes some way to rewrite the whole history[...]

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Wed Mar 09, 2011 3:51 pm I still don't think this is reliable. I wouldn't take anyone's word for it unless it was a scan from a Japanese magazine or from Khara posting something on the internet.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Mar 09, 2011 4:04 pm The original announcement for the 4 movies had the last 2 (movie 3 and 4) be roughly 45 minutes and screened together as 1. The proposed release schedule for that same announcement is way off at this point, so some people think all the info in the announcement can be thrown out the window.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Mar 09, 2011 4:16 pm What Ornette has said is accurate. The production is way off the rails. The original plan made it look like 1.0-3.0 would be re-edits of the TV series, and that Final would be a new ending screened alongside 3.0. It also made it look like the whole thing would be out by what, 2008? Who knows? Will 3.0 and Final come out at the same time? Will they be like two episodes of a whole like 25' and 26'? We'll have to wait and see. An announcement for 3.0 is overdue at this point. It took just shy of two years to produce 2.0, and it's been that long since it was released.

[]:             Quote Post by AkiraT » Wed Mar 09, 2011 4:24 pm Ornette wrote: The original announcement for the 4 movies had the last 2 (movie 3 and 4) be roughly 45 minutes and screened together as 1. The proposed release schedule for that same announcement is way off at this point, so some people think all the info in the announcement can be thrown out the window.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Wed Mar 09, 2011 4:30 pm I guess it's best to just keep your hopes up, right? I mean the Evangelion manga restarted around 2.0's announcement/theater release (I forget, it's been awhile) and the manga is starting back up in April.. so.. Just hope I guess. I mean look at it this way, Khara is most definitely working on the movie as we speak. They don't exactly do much else as an entire studio.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Mar 09, 2011 4:40 pm AkiraT wrote: thanks for info , I did not know had been made an official announcement about this back in 2008. But I have left some doubt because he is the executive director of dynit, and it is strange that will not tip on something like this. but at this point, really... "only time will tell"

[]:             Quote Post by CORE » Thu Mar 10, 2011 9:42 am Anno added "working hard on Q" or some thing to that effect to the end of his Japanese bibliography afue months back. You would think he would say Q+F if they where going to be released to cinema at the same time. I think from this the only likelihood's that are possible are Q in theaters for a time then F in theaters at a later date then Q+F DVD/BD Q in theaters ... this is the end Q DVD/BD The longer they work on Q the less likely F will happen it seems? not knowing if +F will happen or not is obviously deliberate at this point?

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Thu Mar 10, 2011 11:42 am Perhaps Q is a shorthand for 3.0 & FINAL?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Thu Mar 10, 2011 1:03 pm Shogo-Kun wrote: Perhaps Q is a shorthand for 3.0 & FINAL?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 10, 2011 2:29 pm Shogo-Kun wrote: Perhaps Q is a shorthand for 3.0 & FINAL?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Thu Mar 10, 2011 3:23 pm We've literally heard nothing about Q except that it will feature "fan service".

[]: From my take             Quote Post by MelchiorTM » Fri Mar 11, 2011 1:54 am I think what they meant was there are 3 movies (trilogy), and then a whole new ending (Final). Weird way to say it but this might explain the trilogy situation.

[]:             Quote Post by Strayn » Fri Mar 11, 2011 9:18 am I wonder if Anno will ever stick with the original plan instead of remaking his movies over and over again.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Fri Mar 11, 2011 9:50 am Strayn wrote: I wonder if Anno will ever stick with the original plan instead of remaking his movies over and over again.
